questions.stem: A company's support team wants one AI agent to handle order tracking but delegate fee calculations to a separate specialist agent without the customer knowing. Which multi-agent pattern should they use?

options.text: Handoff, because the user needs to interact with the fee specialist directly
Delegation, because the sub-agent performs a background task and returns results to the calling agent
Fan-out, because multiple agents must work on the fee calculation simultaneously

questions.explanation: Delegation is a call-and-return pattern where the parent agent sends a task to a child agent, waits for the result, and continues its own processing. The user does not see or interact with the delegate. Handoff transfers the conversation visibly, and fan-out runs multiple agents in parallel for independent tasks.

questions.stem: A travel company wants their agent to search flights, hotels, and activities simultaneously when a user says 'plan my trip.' Which orchestration pattern enables this?

options.text: Sequential handoff from flights to hotels to activities
Fan-out, where all three search agents run in parallel and return results independently
A single reasoning agent that calls each search tool one at a time

questions.explanation: Fan-out sends tasks to multiple agents simultaneously and aggregates their results. When the user says 'plan my trip,' all three handoff rules match, triggering Flight_Search, Hotel_Search, and Activity_Search in parallel. Each returns results independently via RETURN: true, giving the user a combined trip plan faster than sequential processing.

questions.stem: Agent Platform uses ___ search as its recommended default strategy for knowledge bases, combining vector similarity with keyword matching to balance semantic understanding and exact term precision.
questions.explanation: Hybrid search combines two complementary strategies: semantic search (finds conceptual matches via vector embeddings) and keyword search (finds exact terms like product codes or names). This is the recommended default because it handles both conceptual questions and specific terminology lookups.

questions.stem: According to the Agent Platform FAQ, which statement about deployment options is accurate?

options.text: The platform only runs as a SaaS service managed by ABL
The platform can be self-hosted on your own infrastructure for full control over data residency and security
Self-hosted deployments require a minimum of 64 CPU cores and 256 GB RAM

questions.explanation: The Agent Platform supports self-hosted deployment on your own infrastructure, giving you full control over data residency, security, and scaling. The minimum development requirements are 4 cores and 16 GB RAM. Production recommendations are 16+ cores and 64+ GB RAM, but 256 GB is not a minimum requirement.

questions.stem: A business analyst is deciding whether to use a supervisor or keep a single agent for their customer service system. Which scenario most strongly justifies adding a supervisor?

options.text: The agent needs to answer questions from a single knowledge base about one product line
The system must route users between billing, technical support, and sales specialists, each with different tools and expertise
The company wants to reduce LLM costs by using fewer model calls per conversation

questions.explanation: A supervisor is justified when you have multiple distinct domains requiring different tools, knowledge, and expertise. Routing between billing, technical support, and sales is a classic multi-agent use case. A single product Q&A agent does not need a supervisor, and supervisors actually add routing overhead rather than reducing model calls.
